title: PAMSPEC Evidence Record (0.1-draft)
$comment: R6.1a corrective pass. Records are append-only and immutable. Retraction and supersession are expressed as revision RELATIONSHIPS on a LATER record; the earlier record's claim_status is never rewritten. Intrinsic claim_status covers only how the record's own author characterized their evidence at the moment they wrote it (confirmed / inconclusive / not_testable). Effective disposition of an earlier claim is derived by walking forward and finding a later record with revises.effect in {retracts, supersedes} pointing at it.

record_id.description: Unique, immutable identifier for this record. Referenced by later records via revises.record_id.

recorded_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the record was authored.

test_kind.description: probe = 'what does this subject currently do?'; conformance = 'does this subject satisfy a normative PAMSPEC requirement?'; regression = 'did previously observed behavior change?'; controlled_experiment = 'is the observed signal distinguishable from an experimental artifact?'

requirement_id.description: Dotted identifier of the PAMSPEC requirement being evaluated. Stable across the revision chain — later records that revise an earlier one MUST share the same requirement_id.

subject.description: What was tested. All three fields are required to avoid null identity.

adapter.description: The PAMSPEC adapter through which the subject was observed, if any. Null when the subject is examined directly through its public API (no PAMSPEC adapter in the loop).

spec_commit.description: Full git SHA of the PAMSPEC specification state under which the requirement is evaluated. Never null; requirement text can change while the identifier does not.

profile.description: PAMSPEC profile under evaluation (e.g. 'PAMSPEC-Lite'). Null for architectural probes that do not claim formal profile conformance.

harness_commit.description: Full git SHA of the R7 conformance harness that produced this record. Null when the record was authored without a harness run.

pamspec_context.description: Immutable pointers to the specification and harness context under which this record was produced.

classification.description: Observed outcome. native = subject provides it directly. emulated = the TESTED ADAPTER demonstrably supplied the behavior although the underlying framework did not (never used to describe hypothetical compensation — use adapter_feasibility instead). gap = the requirement is not met and no adapter is in scope. questionable = evidence suggests the requirement itself may be artificial. not_testable = cannot be probed with the current public API without modifying the subject.

claim_status.description: Intrinsic assessment as-written by the author. Retraction and supersession are RELATIONSHIPS carried by later records (see `revises`); they are NOT intrinsic statuses of this record. Effective disposition is derived from the whole chain.

revises.description: Revision relationship to an earlier record. Null when this record is the first for its requirement_id. retracts = the earlier record's claim is wrong. supersedes = the earlier record is replaced without contradiction. reproduces / extends do NOT alter the earlier record's effective disposition.

control_design.description: Generic experimental controls. Required non-null when test_kind='controlled_experiment' (see conditional rule).

allOf.$comment: If classification is not_testable, claim_status must be not_testable.
controlled_experiment requires a non-null control_design object.
not_testable classification requires at least one entry in limitations.
source_review evidence must be paired with an explicit limitation.
